title: Media pipeline: presign -> complete -> worker, row registered at complete
rationale: Presign returns a mediaId + presigned R2 PUT and writes NO db row (bytes go direct to R2, never through the web process). media/complete HEAD-verifies the uploaded object (a presigned PUT cannot bind body length, so size/type are re-checked from storage), registers the listing_media row in processing_status=uploading, then enqueues media-processing AFTER the tx commits. The worker downloads the original, generates variants+blurhash, and flips the row to ready/failed. sort_order=0 is the cover; reorder rewrites 0..n-1.

name: pure-image-core-split-for-testability
description: worker/processors/media-processing.ts exports a pure generateImageVariants(Buffer) that does ALL sharp/blurhash work (responsive webp ladder capped at source width via withoutEnlargement, thumb, blurhash from a 32px raw+ensureAlpha raster) and returns buffers — no R2/DB. The processor wraps it with IO. This lets the acceptance test (real image -> ready webp variants + valid blurhash) run against a real sharp-generated buffer with zero infra, matching the repo convention of pure-unit + availability-gated-integration tests (no module mocking).

content: R2 is driven via the AWS S3 SDK: S3Client { region:'auto', endpoint: https://<R2_ACCOUNT_ID>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com, forcePathStyle:true }, presigned PUTs via @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner (getSignedUrl is fully local — unit-testable with fake creds, no network). Public URLs need a SEPARATE R2_PUBLIC_BASE_URL env (R2 has no deterministic public URL); getR2PublicBaseUrl trims trailing slashes. sharp/blurhash/@aws-sdk were added to package.json (were absent); no pinned deps bumped.
